title: lupinus argenteus subsp. ingratus
intro: Lupinus argenteus subsp. ingratus, commonly known as the silvery lupine, is a subspecies of the perennial lupine plant native to the western United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Lupinus argenteus subsp. ingratus include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the plant's leaves and flowers and steeping them in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
